title: Gated self-improvement: engine proposes taxonomy/method/prompt diffs; human-approved; cannot touch budget/gates/refusals
context: The difference between 'self-improving' and 'self-unbounding' is that ATLTUAE may improve quality (taxonomy, retrieval/verification methods, prompts) but provably cannot modify its own budget caps, gates, or refusal rules - those are governance-controlled and server-enforced. This safety-critical M2 task implements gated self-improvement with human-approved diffs and a red-team case proving the off-limits guarantee.
objective: Implement gated self-improvement: the engine proposes reviewable taxonomy/retrieval/verification/prompt diffs with rationale, applied only after human approval at a checkpoint, and is provably unable (server-enforced + red-team-covered) to propose or apply changes to budget caps, gates, or refusal rules.
acceptanceCriteria: The engine may propose improvements to taxonomy, retrieval/verification methods, and prompts, each as a reviewable diff + rationale, applied only after human approval at a checkpoint
The engine provably cannot propose or apply changes to budget caps, gates, or refusal rules (enforced server-side; covered by a red-team case) - self-improvement is quality-only
Reviewed and signed off by the gate reviewer + maintainer (recorded)
